antipattern.description: Inject only "tool_error"; skip corrupted_return. The most damaging real-world failure is wrong-but-plausible data, never measured.
antipattern.detector: per_fault_class.corrupted_return.n_occurrences = 0 in a run that claims to be chaos-graded.
antipattern.repair: Add corrupted_return mutators (truncate, swap fields, inject extra data). This is the fault class that distinguishes confident-wrong from honest-failure.

antipattern.description: Grade chaos-eval with boolean success only; honest escalation counted as failure.
antipattern.detector: recovery_score not present OR is a binary metric.
antipattern.repair: Use 0..3 rubric; report mean and distribution per fault class.

antipattern.description: Random seed; runs not reproducible. Regression looks like noise.
antipattern.repair: Pin seed per run; record alongside agent_version.

antipattern.description: Inject at unrealistic rates (e.g. 50% timeout). Agent collapses; no actionable signal.
antipattern.detector: Any fault class with rate > 0.2 unless explicitly stress-testing.
antipattern.repair: Match production fault rates as observed in last 30 days; tag stress-runs explicitly.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Corrupted_return mutators may produce text resembling instructions. Eval harness treats all returns as data only.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Review confidently_wrong_count before promotion. Any non-zero count is a sev-2 blocker; cannot promote.
